name: San Francisco Sourdough French Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, sprinkle the dry yeast over warm water and let sit for 5 minutes to dissolve.
2. Stir in sugar, sourdough starter, and gradually add 4 cups of the flour mixture (including salt).
3. Cover the bowl with a damp cloth and let rise at room temperature in a warm, draft-free place for 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
4. Turn the dough onto a floured surface and work in the remaining flour until the dough is no longer sticky. Knead until smooth and silky, about 5 minutes.
5. Shape the dough into one large round or two long loaves. Place on a baking sheet lined with cornmeal.
6. Let rise again at room temperature for 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
7. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Place a shallow pan of water in the lower shelf of the oven.
8. Make diagonal slashes on the top of each loaf.
9. Bake for 40 to 50 minutes, or until the crust is medium-dark brown. Remove from the oven and place on a rack to cool.
